steps.title: Trace what “Consequently” must follow from
steps.content: The passage contrasts tragedies (broad, timeless themes — still widely read and performed) with history plays (require knowledge of several centuries of English history). “Consequently” signals the logical result of that contrast: audiences lacking that historical knowledge will find history plays harder to engage with.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong completions and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘many theatergoers and readers today are likely to find Shakespeare’s history plays less engaging than the tragedies.’: Directly follows from the contrast: history plays demand specialized knowledge tragedies do not, so most audiences find them less engaging. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘some of Shakespeare’s tragedies are more relevant to today’s audiences than twentieth-century plays.’: Twentieth-century plays are never mentioned in the passage, so there is no basis for this comparison. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Romeo and Juliet is the most thematically accessible of all Shakespeare’s tragedies.’: The passage uses Romeo and Juliet as one example of tragedies broadly; it never ranks it above other tragedies. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘experts in English history tend to prefer Shakespeare’s history plays to his other works.’: The passage never mentions historians or their preferences — this introduces information not in the text. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
